trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Visiting journalists, composers and high-schoolers
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 02/23/26

urls.page_title: John: The life and times of the ‘maverick’ Gospel
urls.page_summary: The fourth Gospel of the New Testament holds many of the Bible’s most well-known passages, but also some of its controversies.
urls.page_title: Department of Physics Colloquium, Professor Paul Ginsparg, Cornell University - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
General Physics Colloquium, Professor Paul Ginsparg, Cornell University
Title: The Rise of Slop
Host: Jennet Dickinson
Abstract: The rapid proliferation of AI-generated text, figures, code, and reviews is reshaping the ecology of research co...
urls.page_title: LaFeber-Silbey Lecture considers “A World Without Law?”
urls.page_summary: Scholar of law Philippe Sands will give the LaFeber-Silbey Lecture in History on March 5, considering "Lessons from History and Literature, from Nuremberg to Pinochet and Beyond.”
urls.page_title: How WWII changed ideas of racial purity in Japan
urls.page_summary: The new book explores what happened to “mixed blood” children born to Japanese women and foreign soldiers.
urls.page_title: AASP Wednesday Lunch Series with Wei Gao - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Join us for our Wednesday Lunch Series, featuring guest speakers from Cornell's faculty and staff as well as the surrounding community. Enjoy an informal discussion where you can learn more about the speaker’s work or research, how they ended up d...
urls.page_title: The Eleanor J. and James J. Gibson Lecture in Experimental Psychology - Cornell
urls.page_summary: "Learning to Look and Looking to Learn: Developmental Cascades in Infant Attention" to be presented by Lisa Oakes, Department of Psychology, University California Davis. This is the thirty-fifth in a series of lectures in honor of Eleanor J. Gibs...
urls.page_title: Five from Cornell named 2026 Sloan Research Fellows | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Five Cornell faculty members are among 126 early-career researchers across North America who have won 2026 Sloan Research Fellowships from the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation.
urls.page_title: New summer course to spark civic engagement in high schoolers
urls.page_summary: A Teagle Foundation grant will help fund the course and open pathways to higher education.

The College of Arts & Sciences’ Arts Unplugged series brings research and creative works into the public sphere for discussion and inspiration. These engaging events invite a broad audience to explore the work of scholars, faculty and creative lum...
Awards and honors received by faculty, postdocs and graduate students in the College of Arts & Sciences.
